attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Move the CD that's on top of the desk to on top of the dresser.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a wooden desk with a matching wooden chair positioned in front of it. On the desk's surface, there are several objects:

- Two black mugs, both sitting directly on the desk surface.
- Two CDs (compact discs), both resting on the desk. One is near the center-right, and the other is slightly to its left. Both have a reflective, rainbow-colored surface.
- A black, rectangular, bowl-shaped object (possibly a small tray or container) located near the top-right corner of the desk.
- A black smartphone lying flat on the desk, near the right edge.
- A brown and black digital device (possibly a clock or radio) with a green digital display showing "8:25" and a small stack of books behind it, located on the left side of the desk.
- A yellow-shaded lamp on the far left, illuminating the scene.
- A wooden dresser with three drawers is visible to the right of the desk. The top surface of the dresser is clear except for a single book placed near its back edge.

The desk and dresser are both made of wood with a reddish-brown finish. The floor is tiled with rectangular brown tiles. The wall behind the desk is a light beige color.

In terms of domain-relevant objects:
- The dresser is a receptacle (as it can hold objects on its surface).
- The black bowl-shaped object may be considered a receptacle, but it is not currently holding anything.
- The CDs are objects (not receptacles).
- The smartphone, mugs, digital device, and books are all objects.

No objects are currently in any receptacle (inReceptacle predicate is false for all). No object is being held by an agent (holdsAny is false). No object is marked as clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled. The lamp is on (isOn is true for the lamp, though not in the domain). The robot is not currently at any object’s location (atLocation is false for all objects).

The goal is to move one of the CDs from the desk (its current location) to the top surface of the dresser. The CD to be moved is visually indistinguishable from the other CD, so either can be selected. The dresser is adjacent to the desk and accessible from the robot’s starting position.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem move_cd_to_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        desk - object
        dresser - receptacle
        cd1 - object
        cd2 - object
        mug1 - object
        mug2 - object
        bowl - receptacle
        smartphone - object
        digital_device - object
        book_stack - object
        lamp - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations: all objects are on the desk except the book on dresser
        (atLocation robot desk)
        (inReceptacle cd1 desk)
        (inReceptacle cd2 desk)
        (inReceptacle mug1 desk)
        (inReceptacle mug2 desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (inReceptacle digital_device desk)
        (inReceptacle book_stack desk)
        (inReceptacle lamp desk)
        ; The dresser is a receptacle and has a book on it
        (inReceptacle book_on_dresser dresser)
        ; No object is held
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        ; No object is clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled
        (not (isClean cd1))
        (not (isClean cd2))
        (not (isHot cd1))
        (not (isHot cd2))
        (not (isCool cd1))
        (not (isCool cd2))
        (not (isSliced cd1))
        (not (isSliced cd2))
        (not (isToggled cd1))
        (not (isToggled cd2))
        ; The dresser is not openable (it's a surface, not a container)
        (not (openable dresser))
        ; The bowl is openable (as it's a container-like object)
        (openable bowl)
        (not (opened bowl))
        ; The lamp is on (though not in domain predicates, we don't need to model it)
        ; We assume the robot starts at the desk
        (atLocation robot desk)
    )
    (:goal (and
        ; One of the CDs should be on the dresser
        (inReceptacle cd1 dresser)
        ; The other CD remains on the desk (optional, but implied by moving only one)
        (inReceptacle cd2 desk)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem move_cd_to_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        desk - object
        dresser - receptacle
        cd1 - object
        cd2 - object
        mug1 - object
        mug2 - object
        bowl - receptacle
        smartphone - object
        digital_device - object
        book_stack - object
        lamp - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations: all objects are on the desk except the book on dresser
        (atLocation robot desk)
        (inReceptacle cd1 desk)
        (inReceptacle cd2 desk)
        (inReceptacle mug1 desk)
        (inReceptacle mug2 desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (inReceptacle digital_device desk)
        (inReceptacle book_stack desk)
        (inReceptacle lamp desk)
        ; The dresser is a receptacle and has a book on it
        (inReceptacle book_on_dresser dresser)
        ; No object is held
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        ; No object is clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled
        (not (isClean cd1))
        (not (isClean cd2))
        (not (isHot cd1))
        (not (isHot cd2))
        (not (isCool cd1))
        (not (isCool cd2))
        (not (isSliced cd1))
        (not (isSliced cd2))
        (not (isToggled cd1))
        (not (isToggled cd2))
        ; The dresser is not openable (it's a surface, not a container)
        (not (openable dresser))
        ; The bowl is openable (as it's a container-like object)
        (openable bowl)
        (not (opened bowl))
        ; The lamp is on (though not in domain predicates, we don't need to model it)
        ; We assume the robot starts at the desk
        (atLocation robot desk)
    )
    (:goal (and
        ; One of the CDs should be on the dresser
        (inReceptacle cd1 dresser)
        ; The other CD remains on the desk (optional, but implied by moving only one)
        (inReceptacle cd2 desk)
    ))
)

The scene contains a wooden desk with a matching wooden chair positioned in front of it. On the desk, there are several objects relevant to the task. Two compact discs (CDs) are visible on the desk's surface; one is located near the center-right, and the other is slightly to its left, closer to a black mug. The smartphone and mug are also objects, but they are not the focus of the current instruction.

The robot is initially not located at any object. The instruction requires moving one of the CDs from the desk (its current location) to the top of the dresser. The dresser is not visible in the provided images, but based on the instruction, it is implied to be a separate piece of furniture that can serve as a receptacle. The CD must be picked up from the desk and placed on the dresser.

No objects are currently held by the agent, and no receptacles are opened or toggled. The CDs are not cleaned, heated, cooled, or sliced. The lamp is on, as indicated by the glow, but it is not relevant to the domain predicates. The spatial relationship to note is that both CDs are on the desk, and the desk is the starting location for the CD that needs to be moved. The robot must first navigate to the desk to interact with one of the CDs.

 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

(define (problem move_cd_to_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        desk - receptacle
        dresser - receptacle
        cd1 cd2 - object
        mug - object
        digital_device - object
        lamp - object
        bowl - object
        smartphone - object
        books - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot desk)
        (inReceptacle cd1 desk)
        (inReceptacle cd2 desk)
        (inReceptacle mug desk)
        (inReceptacle digital_device desk)
        (inReceptacle lamp desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (inReceptacle books desk)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (opened desk))
        (not (opened dresser))
        (not (isClean cd1))
        (not (isClean cd2))
        (not (isHot cd1))
        (not (isHot cd2))
        (not (isCool cd1))
        (not (isCool cd2))
        (not (isSliced cd1))
        (not (isSliced cd2))
        (isOn lamp)
        (isToggled lamp)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle cd1 dresser)
        (not (inReceptacle cd1 desk))
    ))
)

(define (problem move_cd_to_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        desk - receptacle
        dresser - receptacle
        cd1 cd2 - object
        mug - object
        digital_device - object
        lamp - object
        bowl - object
        smartphone - object
        books - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot desk)
        (inReceptacle cd1 desk)
        (inReceptacle cd2 desk)
        (inReceptacle mug desk)
        (inReceptacle digital_device desk)
        (inReceptacle lamp desk)
        (inReceptacle bowl desk)
        (inReceptacle smartphone desk)
        (inReceptacle books desk)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (opened desk))
        (not (opened dresser))
        (not (isClean cd1))

        (not (isHot cd2))
